STATE CAPITOL
95814
(916) 445-9781

3600 LIME STREET
SUITE 111
RIVERSIDE, CA 92501
(714) 782-4111

72-811 HIGHWAY 111
PALM DESERT, CA 92260
(619) 340-4488

COMMITTEES
APPROPRIATIONS (CHAIRMAN)
NATURAL RESOURCES AND
WILDLIFE
JUDICIARY
AGRICULTURE AND WATER
RESOURCES

JOINT COMMITTEES
PRISON CONSTRUCTION AND
OPERATIONS (CHAIRMAN)
ORGANIZED RETAIL THEFT
LEGISLATIVE ETHICS
REVISION OF THE PENAL CODE

SELECT COMMITTEES
CHILDREN AND YOUTH (CHAIRMAN)
MOBILE HOMES
PACIFIC RIM (VICE CHAIRMAN)
SMALL BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT
FAIRS & RURAL ISSUES
PLANNING FOR CALIFORNIA'S
GROWTH
SOLID & HAZARDOUS WASTE

California State Senate

STATE SENATOR

ROBERT PRESLEY
THIRTY-SIXTH SENATORIAL DISTRICT

CHAIRMAN
SENATE COMMITTEE ON APPROPRIATIONS

December 5, 1991

Ben Davidian, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
428 "J" Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Davidian:

I am writing to aprise your office about a "turkey giving project"
which I cooperated on with the Southern California Water Company
(Southern).

On Thanksgiving, November 26, 1991, my Riverside office received 150
turkeys purchased by Southern which were distributed by my staff to
the following charitable and/or non-profit organizations:

- Catholic Charities                                50 turkeys
- Salvation Army                                    30 turkeys
- Janet Goeske Center                               20 turkeys
- Casa Blanca Home
  of Neighborly Services                            50 turkeys

While Southern has informed my office that this gift and/or
contribution has been cleared with your office and that I do not need
to present any further documentation, I am nevertheless writing to you
and attaching letters from the above four groups acknowledging their
receipt of the turkeys.

Would you please let me know if this is in compliance with FPPC rules
or regulations?

Thank you very much.

Sincerely,

[signature]

ROBERT PRESLEY
State Senator

RP:jh

sections.question: Would you please let me know if this is in compliance with FPPC rules or regulations?
sections.question_synthetic: A state senator seeks clarification on whether a turkey distribution project conducted in cooperation with Southern California Water Company, where 150 turkeys were distributed to four charitable organizations, complies with FPPC rules and regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter from a state official to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No formal conclusion or advice section exists. The document contains the senator's account of the turkey distribution event and a request for FPPC guidance on compliance. Attachments referenced but not included in provided text.

embedding.summary: State Senator Robert Presley writes to FPPC Chairman Ben Davidian to inquire whether a turkey giving project involving 150 turkeys purchased by Southern California Water Company and distributed to four charitable organizations complies with FPPC regulations.
